OFFICES OF

THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF ORANGE

ADRIAN KUYPER
COUNTY COUNSEL

P-297
B-710

WILLIAM J. McCOURT
CHIEF ASSISTANT

10 CIVIC CENTER PLAZA
MAILING ADDRESS: P.O. BOX 1379
SANTA ANA, CALIFORNIA 92702-1379

ARTHUR C. WAHLSTEDT, JR.
LAURENCE M. WATSON
ASSISTANTS

714-834-3300
FAX 714-834-2359

Winter's Direct Dial Number

714/

834-3943

VICTOR T. BELLERUE
JOHN P. BROWN
EDWARD N. DURAN
ERVYC BLACK
RICHARD D. OVIEDO
BENJAMIN P. DE MAYO
DIANA L. HAGEN
GENE AXELROD
ROBERT L. AUSTIN
DONALD H. RUBIN
DAVID P. CHAFFEE
GREGORY L. COWAN
BARBARA L. STOCKER
JAMES F. MEADE
STEPHEN H. WEISS
DAVID BEALES
ELIZABETH A. DAVIS
JAMES L. TURNER
NICHOLAS S. CHRISOS
THOMAS F. MORSE

WANDA S. FLORENCE
HOPE SNYDER
THOMAS C. AGIN
MERRIE A. JOHANSSEN
SUSAN M. NILSEN
SARA L. PARKER
LINDA J. BERGUND
KARYN J. DRIESSEN
KATHY PAUL
KAREN R. PRATHER
J. DAN LILLEY
RICARDO J. LOPEZ
JIM PERSINGER
GEOFFREY K. HUNT
JACK W. GOLDER
DEBORAH M. GALEINER
DAPHENE A. MILLER
ROBERT G. OVERBY
PAMELA KEMP
LISA PESKY MALMSTEN

DEPUTIES

February 5, 1991

California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Susan Barber, Legal Division

Re: Limitation On Payments By Third Parties For
Travel And Accommodations For Local Elected
Officials

Ladies and Gentlemen:

We have noted that your 1990-91 "Statement of Economic
Interests" Manual warns that payment for travel and accommodations
to elected officeholders in connection with attendance, speeches or
other appearances at conferences, meetings or social events is
subject to the new annual limit of $1,000.

Out of an abundance of caution we have so advised our clients.
However, as pointed out in a January 28, 1991 telephone discussion
between the undersigned and Susan Barber, this office does not
agree with such a broad interpretation. Although Government Code
Section 89501 limits local elected office holders to $1,000 per
calendar year for any gift or honorarium from a single source,
gifts of travel and accommodations in connection with attendance at
meetings, conferences and the like are specifically exempted from
the limit.

We understand that the travel expenses limitation does apply
to state officials. We also understand that travel and
accommodation payments of $50 or more must still be reported and
payments of $250 or more will ordinarily require disqualification.

California Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 5, 1991
Page 2


However, pursuant to our January 30, 1991 discussion, we ask
that you confirm our opinion that the $1,000 annual limit on
honoraria for speeches or attendance at conferences, meetings or
other gatherings does not apply to reimbursement for travel and
reasonable subsistence for local elected office holders.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation.

Very truly yours,

ADRIAN KUYPER, COUNTY COUNSEL

By ________________________
Terry C. Andrus, Deputy

TCA:vn
